results.title: 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters
results.snippet: # 10-Year Treasury Bond Yield: What It Is and Why It Matters. These rates indicate the confidence of investors in the economy. The 10-year Treasury note yield is commonly used as a measure of the U.S. government’s 10-year borrowing rate. It serves as a benchmark for other interest rates and is a key indicator of investor sentiment about economic conditions. ## Understanding 10-Year Treasury Bond Yields. The importance of the 10-year Treasury bond yield goes beyond just understanding the return on investment (ROI) for the security. Prices for the 10-year bond drop when confidence is high, which causes yields to rise. This confidence factor is also felt outside of the U.S., as it points to the future of the global economy. The longer the Treasury bond's time to maturity, the higher the rates
